mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates signal deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings visibility, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP and industrial production signal a contraction in aggregate demand, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both GDPC1 and INDPRO decline.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising federal funds rates above 4.5%, increase discount rates and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strong manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal robust demand for residential construction, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST rises.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation, indicated by CPIAUCSL above 3.0%, increases real interest rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment and declines in unemployment signal a tightening labor market that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls.
